key_insight: Integrating differentiable finite element analysis into stellarator coil optimization lets designers co-optimize coil shape and support clamp placement for both magnetic performance and structural safety.
engineering_relevance: This approach can shorten the trial-and-error design cycle for fusion reactors by reducing stress in brittle HTS coils without sacrificing field quality.
method: Embed support-structure FEA inside the stellarator coil optimization loop via coil-fem.
Optimize coil geometry and clamp locations jointly using a stress penalty term.
Balance magnetic field error, coil-body stress, and geometric simplicity in one workflow.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a design with 2.4x lower RMS von Mises stress and similar field error compared with an unoptimized baseline.
watch_item: Results depend on the fidelity of the differentiable structural model and may require careful validation against full engineering FEA and real hardware constraints.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
